drink.name: Shaken Espresso
drink.reason: Specifically recommended by staff and enjoyed by a 5-star reviewer with almond milk. Other coffee mentions are generic ('rich coffee', 'strong coffee'), making this the only named drink with positive specific feedback.
drink.quote: recommended me the shaken espresso (which I enjoyed w/almond milk!)

food.name: Bagel
food.reason: Bagels are the standout item, praised across multiple 5-star reviews as the best in Manhattan, hot, and perfectly prepared. Even the 1-star reviewer admitted the bagel tasted good.
food.quote: the best in Manhattan... way better than the big-name chain places

reviewSample.text: The bagels here are honestly the best in Manhattan. They’re way better than the big-name chain places that got famous. I’m not sure if someone named Erick is the owner, but he’s super energetic and really knows how to make customers feel welcome. Both the taste and the vibe are amazing—definitely give these bagels a try!

The music selection was so good, remixes of RnB, good vibes.. staff was very helpful on coffee recommendations, they were super nice and welcoming! Leo and I forget the other guys name who recommended me the shaken espresso (which I enjoyed w/almond milk!)

Also had the French toast combo w/scrambled eggs and bacon!

This little coffee shop may be small and quaint, but the flavors are anything but. From the rich coffee to the amazing breakfast options, everything is made with care and quality ingredients. The homemade cream cheese is absolutely unforgettable and easily one of the highlights of the menu. Cozy atmosphere, great food, and friendly service make this a spot well worth visiting.

drink.name: Specialty latte
drink.reason: Reviewers praise the specialty lattes and coffee as beautifully crafted and well-paired with pastries, though no single signature drink is named repeatedly.
drink.quote: specialty lattes are also fantastic and beautifully crafted

food.name: Matcha cream puff
food.reason: Cream puffs (especially matcha) are the most praised item across multiple high-star reviews, called the best in NYC with generous fillings and rich, balanced cream.
food.quote: exceeded my expectations! Crusty on the outside and generous with the fillings

reviewSample.text:
We tried the hot chocolate, matcha cream puff, matcha croissant, and lavender latte at Bibble & Sip.
The matcha croissant was the standout—flaky, buttery, and filled with a rich matcha cream that wasn’t overly sweet. The matcha cream puff was also delicious, light, and well balanced. The hot chocolate was comforting and satisfying, perfect for a cozy break.
The only miss for us was the lavender latte—the floral notes were a bit too strong for our taste. Everything else, though, was beautifully made and clearly high quality. It does get busy and seating is limited, but it’s still a great stop if you love matcha-forward desserts. We’d definitely come back to try more from the menu.

The best cream puffs in NYC! 🧁 The Earl Grey flavor is a game-changer—crispy on the outside and overflowing with silky cream inside. Their specialty lattes are also fantastic and beautifully crafted. Super cute vibe and great for anyone with a sweet tooth. Highly recommend stopping by if you’re near the Theater District!

Will be revisiting this dessert shop after 2-3years which was about 3years ago. Love to drop by and have a piece of heaven whenever I visit NYC. Bought the matcha cream puff and it exceeded my expectations! Crusty on the outside and generous with the fillings! Super yummy for dessert lovers like me 😍 (pic were taken during my first visit).

drink.name: Flat White
drink.reason: Specifically praised as rich and smooth in a 5-star review; other coffee mentions were generic or average.
drink.quote: The flat white is rich, smooth, and full of flavor

food.name: Chocolate Chip Cookie
food.reason: Overwhelmingly the signature item, praised across nearly every review as warm, gooey, and award-winning—best in NYC.
food.quote: warm, gooey, and easily the best in the city

reviewSample.text: Okay..I was a tad skeptical to pay $6.50 for one chocolate chip cookie…BUT it was worth it. Sooo warm ooey gooey and melt in your mouth yummy! I wish I would have tried more things there but I was in a rush. I will definitely be stopping back in next time I’m in the city. Best cookies I have had there thus far!

Culture Espresso is a cozy coffee spot!
Famous of their good cookies 🍪.
The flat white is rich, smooth, and full of flavor, and their drinks are always made with care.
The chocolate chip cookies are incredible — warm, gooey, and easily the best in the city.

The café has a cozy vibe with friendly staff and great service. Perfect place to grab a quality coffee or take a break in the city.

I came here because the reviews were great and Reddit pointed me in this direction. I had an Americano and a chocolate chip cookie. The Americano was average - not great, but not bad by any means. The cookie (which apparently has won a lot of awards) was slightly above average. Maybe it's because it wasn't warm, but I got my order for dine-in so I would have assumed they'd warm it up before giving it to me on a plate.

Overall, a good stop in but I would never go out of my way to go here.

drink.name: Osmanthus latte
drink.reason: Mentioned by multiple reviewers as a popular signature drink; described as smooth, though best ordered hot for fuller flavor.
drink.quote: unique latte spots around SoHo

food.reason: No food item received positive specific praise. Egg bites were served cold, cookie butter was disappointing, and pastries were only called 'fine'.

reviewSample.text:
Great vibes but questionable mixing mechanics.

My friend and I were looking for unique latte spots around SoHo, and this place came up. We stopped by late afternoon on a weekend, and the café was absolutely packed with people working. We grabbed seats at the bar and ordered their popular osmanthus latte and honey rose latte, both iced.

The lattes themselves were solid, but because we got them iced, it felt like the honey and syrups didn’t fully integrate into the coffee. The first 80% of the drink was just generic latte, and we kept swapping cups trying to figure out which was which. It wasn't until we hit the bottom of the cup that the flavors finally woke up and punched us in the face. I’d recommend ordering these hot if you want a more even flavor from start to finish.

Stopped by The Lost Draft for an Osmanthus latte - it was smooth but too mild to really catch the osmanthus flavor. The café has a warm, earthy vibe with plenty of seating. It’s a cozy spot to unwind or get some work done.

I’ve been searching for a sesame latte to top the black sesame latte from Tadaima, and the Lost Draft now has my second favorite. The sesame is wonderfully blended and adds an earthy, caramelized flavor to the latte.

This is also a great place to work, having good WiFi, outlets, lots of seating, and a bathroom.

drink.name: Cappuccino
drink.reason: Espresso drinks are praised for richness and balance; cappuccino specifically called out as a good kick to start the day. Coffee broadly described as 'chef's kiss'.
drink.quote: Good amount of kick to start my day

food.name: Buttermilk pancakes
food.reason: Multiple reviewers rave about the pancakes with toasted hazelnuts, calling them perfectly balanced and not too sweet. Stronger and more consistent praise than the eggs benny.
food.quote: hit a perfect balance on flavors and are not at all too sweet

reviewSample.text:
A very cozy and busy brunch location in midtown. They don’t take reservations so we had to wait 30 minutes but it was well worth the wait. The eggs Benny was very tasty and the bread has the perfect amount of crunch. The French toast was one of the best I’ve ever had. The espresso drinks had a nice richness and not too acidic.

Taking off one star because the male hostess who was seating people was extremely rude when we asked about wait time. All the other staff were lovely and helpful.

I was craving pancakes, and found this spot near our place. Good thing, this was close by and the wait wasn’t too long!

While waiting, I ordered a cappuccino. Good amount of kick to start my day. We got seated after maybe 10 minutes? Order my pancakes and my husband ordered the eggs benny.

Pancakes were good, and it didn’t need syrup. I liked that it had toasted hazelnuts! Couldn’t finish all three, so my husband finished the last one. The eggs benny was good too. Hollandaise could be a bit more tangy in taste, but that is just my preference. The potatoes that came with it were 👌🏽👌🏽👌🏽 Ordered a cup of drip coffee. It was a good end to the meal.

Would come back!

The buttermilk pancakes (despite having honeycomb) hit a perfect balance on flavors and are not at all too sweet! The toasted hazelnuts and banana really pulled through nice flavors without overpowering.

The shakshuka had some nice zing to it, but a bit heavy on the silantro. Same high quality ingredients cooked with care for the other dishes too. Overall, whatever you pick on the menu you’ll be very happy!

The coffee is “chefs kiss”, and the hot chocolate too is not too sweet and just great quality. The wait is probably a bit too long to become my go to takeaway spot but in terms of quality it’s right at the top of my list in NYC.

The service felt a bit rushed and inexperienced. We asked for a table and were given a time to return, but then someone else had been given it and without an ounce of embarrassment were asked to wait 25 mins again. There’s also a lot of movement happening collecting dishes etc for not a lot of output, e.g. they don’t make eye contact etc in the process so it’s hard to ask for things. The waiting staff’s quality felt especially surprising given the contrast when compared to the focussed execution of both the baristas and kitchen staff.

However the service is made up for by a cozy ambience, great soundtrack (and appropriate volume) and comfortable lighting and chatter noise.

Can strongly recommend but hope they can work together on raising the professionalism bar of the waiting staff.

drink.name: Strawberry Matcha Latte
drink.reason: Mentioned positively in multiple reviews, praised as 'amazing and refreshing' with customization options.
drink.quote: real strawberry matcha latte. It was amazing and refreshing

food.reason: Only the croissant was mentioned for food, and it received negative feedback ('very mid', needs rebaking).

reviewSample.text: I thought that a 5-star rating only happened when the owner rated its own establishment. However, I was wrong. This little coffee shop has many reviews and a perfect, well-deserved score. The house blend is medium toast with the flavor of something closer to dark roast; exquisite. The staff is very courteous and attentive. The site is clean, the espresso machine looks very clean, and the presentation is top notch with some type of latte art.

The real chocolate latte was delicious! You can tell they use real chocolate and the grated chocolate on top is a nice aesthetic and flavorful choice. Would be my go to spot if I lived here! My husband got the strawberry matcha latte which was also really good!!

Great Americano, not acidic or burnt.
Croissant was very mid. Not crunchy or airy. Cold tasting and need to be rebaked. I suggest take it home and rewarm with oven

drink.name: Pour over
drink.reason: Specifically praised as 'on point' and recommended over the drip; aligns with the café's single-roast philosophy noted by another reviewer.
drink.quote: Had a really good pour over. It was on point.

food.name: Cardamom bun
food.reason: Named favorite across multiple reviews, praised for unique spice-forward flavor and great chew.
food.quote: this cardamom bun was my favourite of them all

reviewSample.text: The pastries are delicious and the croissants are definitely my favorite. I crave them all the time. Coffee is great too. It’s been pretty consistently busy every time I’ve gone so I never expect to actually be able to sit down. Usually just grab and go. Their branding is on point, too. Love the goat on the boxes, cups, etc.

Came here based on recommendations from social media. It did not disappoint!!! The line was basically out the door but thankfully it moved pretty quick and we only ordered pastries. Ordered the croissant, sourdough, canele and cardumum bun. All FANTASTIC!!!!! That crossiant took me back to Europe and that bun was a little sweet but had a great chew. The candle was a surprise but still excellent. I could eat these pastries without feeling guilty or full to the point that I was over saturated with too many calories. It was amazing!

As someone from Denmark, La Cabra has always been a familiar refuge — a place where coffee is treated with the same quiet rigour and curiosity that shaped its beginnings in Aarhus. Their approach is built on intention: one roast profile, chosen at the cupping table, meant to reveal the coffee’s character rather than manipulate it.

I wasn’t expecting a queue for something that feels so intrinsically like home, but that’s New York asserting itself. The space was lovely, the coffee tasted exactly as it should — clean, bright, and grounded in the philosophy they’ve carried from Aarhus to Copenhagen to here — yet the rush of the café stole a bit of the calm I usually associate with La Cabra. More of the city’s energy than the café’s fault, but still noticeable.

Still, it was a comfort to sit there, coffee in hand, knowing the same careful decisions underpin every cup on both sides of the Atlantic.

drink.name: Flat white
drink.reason: Specifically praised as a go-to in a 5-star review from a 15-year regular, with espresso quality also highlighted across multiple reviews.
drink.quote: My go-to order is small flat white

food.reason: No food items are mentioned in any of the reviews.

reviewSample.text: Anna and Salem we're amazing, I'm a newbie in coffee but ended enjoying a delicious espresso.   The passion and menu knowledge they showed  inspired me to try their Black sesame matcha that was also delicious and perfectly balanced. Will def be back!!!
Have been coming for more than 15 years. Consistent and  delicious espresso coffee from Portland. My go-to order is small flat white. One of the most reliable coffees in New York City

Hey friends! This review is about sustainability only.

By 2050, we will have more plastic in the ocean than fish. While I so appreciate that your shop offers real reusable mugs, I was saddened to see how unclear that option is for customers who are enjoying their coffee in-house.

I wonder if there are ways you can better communicate the availability of reusables to folks who are buying coffee to stay here.

Maybe that means putting a sign at the cash register, or maybe that means incorporating an explicit ask from your baristas.

But it feels such a shame to see all of these coffees getting enjoyed in-house, and then see these cups get discarded right into the trash after 30 minutes of use, to head straight to a landfill, the oceans, or incinerator, and ultimately as micro plastics in our bodies.

The plastic waste crisis will need both the consumer and the provider to help lessen its impact. Thank you for considering my note and for empowering New Yorkers to be better to our local ecosystem and our global ecosystem.

To those reading this: you can get a mug at the shop by explicitly asking for one.

drink.name: Pourover
drink.reason: Reviewer specifically recommends the pourover, and others praise the rare/special bean selection that makes pourovers shine here.
drink.quote: definitely go for the pourover

food.name: Duh Donuts
food.reason: Explicitly recommended by name as a must-try vegan treat available at the shop.
food.quote: Definitely try the donuts if they're available- they're from Duh Donuts

reviewSample.text:
Super fun coffee program featuring some of the best roasters out there. Great community centered cafe. Owner is knowledgable, kind , and has put together a great team.

Definitely a destination if Gowanus is out of your way. If you’re a local this should be one of your regular stops.

Love this place! Finally visited for the first time today & can’t believe I haven’t been here yet! It’s super bike friendly with tools and pumps and events. They have fabulous drinks and vegan treats (it’s all vegan!). Definitely try the donuts if they’re available- they’re from Duh Donuts & they have a bunch of gluten free/vegan snacks too from other vegan bakers. It’s a huge space so plenty of space to work &/or hang out and enjoy your drink in a real cup/mug.

As hipster as it gets - but I love it! Such a cozy, relaxing and genuine space with friendly staff and quirky decoration. It’s spacious and feels like part workshop part bike shop and part living room. Make no mistake, they are serious about their coffee with an exquisite selection of special, rare and eclectic beans. It’s not cheap but definitely go for the pourover.

drink.name: Sticky Bun Latte / Honey Nut Latte
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers rave about specialty lattes; Honey Nut Latte called '10/10' and Sticky Bun Latte was a staff-recommended standout.
drink.quote: Honey Nut Latte is a 10/10

food.name: Chocolate almond croissant
food.reason: Pastries are widely praised, with the chocolate almond croissant getting a specific strong recommendation; cheese danish also highly praised but croissant called out explicitly.
food.quote: I definitely recommend the chocolate almond croissant

reviewSample.text:
Drip drop Café is one of my absolute favorite places to get a beverage. they always have a fun menu. They have all the basics like lattes and coffee. What I like is that they can put a spin on something if you’re interested in it. I have tried everything from a coconut frozen lemonade to an iced blueberry Matcha to a strawberry shortcake Matcha. My other favorite thing about drip drop is the service. Everyone is really friendly, and they don’t have long wait times. My absolute favorite person is Apollo. If he’s working on your order, you are in great hands! The best thing is if you don’t know what you want, everyone there will recommend something and it’ll taste great.

Also, they have pastries. I definitely recommend the chocolate almond croissant. They have indoor and outdoor seating if you want to enjoy your drink on site.
Lovely cosy cafe with a warm, inviting atmosphere. The coffee was smooth and delicious, and I especially loved the muffin and donut. Perfect little spot to relax.

This is my new favorite coffee shop stop spot in SoHo. Friendly staff, very warm & welcoming. Nice amount of seating & refreshments that do not disappoint.

I had a large & delicious 3 shot latte with regular milk & it really was perfection. I also tried the cheese danish & was pleasantly surprised. The pastry was lightly sweet with the perfect amount of pastry cheese cream & the bread had a croissant flakiness on the outside with a slight chew moistness towards the inside. Very well balanced & the perfect accompaniment to the milky latte. It did not disappoint. Everything was on point. Give yourself a treat & visit this place.

Only slightly irritating thing was when I sat in the back & I couldn’t get a strong signal on my phone. There is free WiFi but you have to ask for the barista to give you the login information. They print it for you on a small piece of paper that gives you 1 hour, after that you pay $6 an hour. I completely understand, it’s to keep people from camping out, frees up seats, etc. But, why not post the WiFi information? It could still then automatically closeout after an hour, and it will keep people from finding out after the fact that they need to go request the WiFi information. I had to leave my food at the table, go to the front to request WiFi information & rush back to stop the table cleaner, who was literally walking away with my coffee, “Stop please! I’m not finished!”🤣

*Prices are competitive $15.03, for a pastry, a large 3 shot latte with milk & a 15% tip

drink.name: Iced coffee
drink.reason: Specifically praised as one of the best ever, with rotating fruity roasts and served with complimentary sparkling water for a unique tasting experience.
drink.quote: One of the best iced coffees I've ever had

food.reason: Only one review vaguely mentions 'pastries are always fresh' with no specific item named. Sey is focused on coffee, not food.

reviewSample.text:
A visit to Sey Coffee is nothing short of a pilgrimage for anyone serious about specialty coffee. This is a world-renowned roastery whose coffees are celebrated in some of the best specialty cafés globally — including Motors Coffee — and experiencing it at the source is something special.

The top-shelf espressos were absolutely mind-blowing. Clean, expressive, layered — every shot showcased precision and a deep respect for the coffee itself. You can taste the intention behind every detail.

A special thank you to Daniel and Lorenzo for making my visit unforgettable. Their knowledge, hospitality, and genuine passion elevated the entire experience.

Sey continues to set the bar for what modern specialty coffee can be.

There is no hype behind this coffee shop, hyperbole over the quality of exacting standards in this veritable laboratory where cups of coffee are constructed. Plant lined walls and ceilings reminiscent of your favorite rain forest. While lines may at times seem lengthy, to cue for coffee is indeed worth the wait for a liquid masterpiece. Friendly staff, plethora of flavors and alternatives -hot dark chocolate for the non-coffee drinkers. Delicious and chill without laptops or wifi -come to drink and socialize

One of the best iced coffees I’ve ever had.

Everything here seemed to revolve around the roast. No trendy drinks, no syrups, just coffee and tea. They rotate the roast for their iced coffee, and the one they were using when I went was incredibly fruity. Even served cold, it maintained onto that bright acidity you usually only get from a well-made filtered coffee.

The best part was that they serve it with a free cup of sparkling water. Taking a sip of the coffee and then a sip of the sparkling water added this unexpected dimension. Almost like a deconstructed espresso tonic. For whatever reason, the fizz gave it a lightly sweet finish that wasn’t there on its own. I tried pouring a bit of the sparkling water into the coffee at the end, but it didn’t hit the same.

drink.name: Cookies and cream matcha
drink.reason: Specifically praised as 10/10 in a 5-star review; other matcha (daydream) was criticized.
drink.quote: the cookies and cream matcha was definitely a 10/10

food.name: Breakfast pastries
food.reason: Only specific food mention, called 'fantastic' in a 5-star review, but just one mention.
food.quote: Fantastic breakfast pastries and excellent coffee

reviewSample.text: I literally come here everyday because of the atmosphere. They are quick and they are so sweet especially Jen & Chloé. I definitely recommend spending your coin here, you will not be disappointed!

I love this Blank Street location! Fantastic breakfast pastries and excellent coffee, and a nice atmosphere to sit and chill at if there’s a free table. I’m a regular here and the entire team is great, like Jen who is so friendly and always anticipates my order. It’s not easy having to be on your game everyday and so early in the morning, and the crew here keeps things running as smooth as ever. Big thanks for being so awesome! Definitely worth popping in if you need a caffeine refuel.

I ordered the daydream matcha (honey, vanilla bean, cinnamon) and honestly, I tasted more honey than matcha :/ And I couldn’t taste the vanilla bean or cinnamon either unfortunately—it tasted kind of like cereal milk??

If you want to still try this cafe, maybe try a different drink lol

drink.name: Tiramisu Latte
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers specifically praise it as smooth, comforting, and delicious, with the biscuit topping making it feel like a drinkable dessert.
drink.quote: smooth and comforting...like a drinkable dessert

food.name: Espresso Chocolate loaf
food.reason: Specifically called 'absolutely delicious' in a 5-star review. Other food mentions (Basque cheesecake, sugar cookie) are also positive but this had the strongest specific praise.
food.quote: absolutely delicious Espresso Chocolate loaf

reviewSample.text: I tried a few of their February specials and everything felt thoughtfully made, not overly sweet or artificial.The Tiramisu Latte was smooth and comforting, especially with the little biscuit on top that made it feel like a drinkable dessert. The Strawberry Matcha Cloud was light and fresh, with real fruit flavor instead of syrupy sweetness. Their Rose Velvet Latte had a subtle floral note that was surprisingly calming. I also loved the Basque cheesecakes, especially the lavender earl grey, which was unique without being overpowering. The space has a warm wood-toned interior that’s perfect for working or catching up with a friend!

I was so excited to try this cafe when I discovered it on Instagram! The speciality lattes that I wanted to try were out of stock, so I opted for the Tiramisu latte! Goodness, it was so delicious! It was warm to taste! And my sister ate the sugar cookie! Would love to come back again! However, the cafe is quite small!

retro cafe in Nolita, NYC, on mulberry street, @urbanbackyardnyc has a great retro feel. It’s cosy, has friendly barista, great coffee and lovely baked pastries. The basement has a lot of comfortable seating.

drink.name: Cortado
drink.reason: Specifically praised as delicious in a 5-star review; other coffee mentions are generic.
drink.quote: The cortado was delicious

food.name: Marble Cake
food.reason: Only specifically named food item, described as solid. Other reviews mention pastries/baked goods generically.
food.quote: Marble Cake - solid

reviewSample.text: What an amazing specialty coffee shop here in Brooklyn. The cortado was delicious and I also got a hot chocolate for the kids. The staff were so friendly and kind. The service was extremely fast. They also were generous and gave me some extra cups to give out the hot chocolate to my kids in different cups. There is some space to sit and work or meet but the place was already pretty packed back late morning. I’d highly recommend coming here if you like good espresso and specialty coffee

I went to this coffee shop several times when I was in Brooklyn. The staff is nice, there's ample seating inside, plus there's an outdoor courtyard plus a restroom! Their coffee drinks were excellent and they had good pastries too. It's a great place to hang out and enjoy breakfast or lunch.

So happy to have such a nice cafe in the neighborhood. One of my to do’s when first moving here was to find a good coffee spot and Hamlet is now my go-to.

There’s a good amount of seating though I’d still recommend going early to find a spot. It’s great for doing a bit of work.

Coffee selection is minimal and solid. Also tried a baked good for the first time and loved it!

drink.name: Espresso
drink.reason: Multiple high-star reviews specifically praise the espresso for its smooth, clear taste notes; the salted caramel latte was called just 'fine.'
drink.quote: Their espresso is so smooth, I barely add any cream and sugar

food.name: Bacon Cheddar Danish
food.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as 'delightful,' with the reviewer wanting more. The onion focaccia was noted as very greasy.
food.quote: Got a bacon cheddar Danish and it was delightful

reviewSample.text: This is the best little coffee shop on the Upper West Side! Cozy and chill with a wonderful and friendly staff. Their espresso is so smooth, I barely add any cream and sugar, so yum! Solid State has merch as well with cute hoodies and hats. Plenty of seating in the back and a comfy waiting space. They are super fast too, so efficient and quick. Great music and vibes, I seriously look forward to going here when I visit New York. Great location, just 2 blocks from Central Park in a safe area surrounded by brownstones. I love this place and can’t wait to go back! Thanks and keep up the great work!

One of the best coffee ive had during the NYC trip. The taste note was solid and clear not just plain bitter nothing. You can taste the tastenote as described. The staffs were also friendly and hospitable. There were also various of beans to choose from

Great coffee, crowded space on the weekends! A little pricey, but that didnt deter folks... the line was practically out the door!

Got a bacon cheddar Danish and it was delightful. Had to stop myself from getting more than one pastry.

Be forewarned, the reviews are true: there's no wifi here. But the comfy seats and outlets make it a perfect spot to so offline work or catch up with friends

drink.name: Huellas del Jaguar Gesha drip
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review as amazing and fruit forward; drip coffee broadly mentioned positively across reviews.
drink.quote: huellas del jaguar gresha drip and it was amazing

food.name: Sandwiches
food.reason: Only one review mentions food, generically calling sandwiches good. Not enough specific praise to recommend a particular item confidently.
food.quote: Good coffee and sandwiches

reviewSample.text: I didn’t try the pour over but they do have them! I used chat gpt to find a pour over spot and went to a different coffee shop that didn’t end up having it. This location does tho! The area has some construction but the interior is nice. The drip is allegedly as good as a pour over with a nice machine (Bonavita? Can’t remember) making the coffee. I didn’t think it tasted as flavorful or well rounded as a pour over but I would enjoy another cup of drip, to go, if in a rush. Thanks Scout for the great service!

Such a cute, homey place. Dropped in on a snowy winter day and was welcomed with a smile and a napkin to wipe my glasses fog (so sweet!)

I got the huellas del jaguar gresha drip and it was amazing! Very fruit forward, warm and light — good for a beginner coffee drinker like myself.

As a matcha fiend myself, the fact they also serve and sell Ippodo matcha means they KNOW quality.

On a snowy Sunday, there was a decent amount of seating available as people came and went. I sat at the low top 6-seater with a few other folks and they were nice and made space. There is not a bathroom though!

Highly recommend!
Wonderful staff - friendly and helpful. Good coffee and sandwiches. Very comfortable place to get coffee, food, and hang out. Enjoy.

drink.name: Orange Spice Latte
drink.reason: Mentioned by name in two separate 5-star reviews as a standout, with one reviewer getting it to-go and another calling it a favorite.
drink.quote: orange spice hot latte

food.name: Honey Cake
food.reason: Specifically praised as 'sooo yummy' and noted as gluten free in a 5-star review. Other food mentions (chocolate coconut dessert, almond orange cake) each appear only once as well, but honey cake received the most enthusiastic specific praise.
food.quote: the honey cake I ordered was sooo yummy

reviewSample.text:
This has to be one of my favorite cafes I’ve ever been to! I was on my way home from a trip to NY and wanted to try a new cafe and I am so happy I chose this one. It was overwhelming trying to choose since there’s so many in Brooklyn, but I’m so glad I chose this one. It was easy for me to find street parking as well!

The baristas were so sweet, the latte flavors were so unique and delicious, and the honey cake I ordered was sooo yummy and gluten free! I ordered their rose latte, and I had to get their orange spice latte to go. The place is small, so it’s a little hard to get a seat, but it makes for such a sweet, cozy energy. I chatted with two ladies while I enjoyed my coffee, and it was so wholesome. I can’t wait to come back next time I’m in Brooklyn🩷.

What a sweet oasis in the midst of the busy Brooklyn buzz! This small, rustic, cozy cafe had all the feels. Sipping on a hot latte on their comfy couch, during a snowstorm, was the warmest treat. This cafe was literally the emotional hug I didn’t know I needed. The barista named “MAY” was an absolute delight! A pleasure! She treated every patron with respect & patience, and she made every drink with LOVE. My fayv was the iced shaken espresso, the orange spice hot latte, and the London Fog hot latte - made from fresh earl gray tea leaves. I highly recommend this spot, either for a date, a quick pastry& coffee, a space to work at, or even just to take some cute pics. Just be prepared, it’s a very small space. Enjoy! ✌️❤️

I love the cozy and casual setting of the coffee shop! You think you’re in your living room with friends! The space is small but hip and comfortable! It has a warm and great ambiance. The decor is absolutely original! We enjoyed delicious hot and iced coffee, as well as a very delicious chocolate coconut dessert!

drink.name: Drip coffee
drink.reason: Specifically praised as the best drip coffee in Manhattan, with multiple reviewers calling the coffee fantastic and well-made.
drink.quote: Best drip coffee I've had in Manhattan

food.name: Lamington
food.reason: Specifically called out as a perfect adult pastry, not too sweet and perfectly made. Croissant got mixed reviews (one called it not fresh).
food.quote: perfect adult pastry breakfast because it's not too sweet and perfectly made

reviewSample.text: Very busy FiDi coffee shop serving pastries as well as breakfast. Menu includes eggs, pastries, healthy options, etc. relatively cramped seating but nicely designed inside. Service is very friendly and most importantly they have fantastic coffee. Best drip coffee I've had in Manhattan. They have a cake/pastry called a Lamington that is a perfect adult pastry breakfast because it's not too sweet and perfectly made. No photo because I ate it too quickly. So why not five stars? A 16 oz drip coffee is $6.25. That's not okay even for Manhattan.
Just a totally great experience! The heated croissant was delicious. The coffee was superb. Service was so fast. I couldn't believe it. The avocado toast and eggs was delicious. Great vibe.

High quality coffee drinks that aren't weak or bitter. They have a great selection and staff is amazing. It's nice to find a coffee shop in Manhattan with ample seating, a restroom, amazing drinks and talented staff. One of my favorite coffee places in the city by far. The perfect spot to meet up with friends.

drink.name: Deconstructed latte
drink.reason: The deconstructed latte is the cafe's signature drink and was specifically praised as 'an experience in itself.' While the anaerobic Colombian pourover also received high praise, the deconstructed latte is the more distinctive, signature offering unique to this shop.
drink.quote: rich, smooth, and beautifully balanced

food.name: Almond croissant
food.reason: Only food item specifically named, praised in a 5-star review with detail about texture and staff warming it up.
food.quote: crisp outside, soft and fragrant inside—no sogginess

reviewSample.text:
One of the best pourovers I've had in a cafe in a long time! Loved this cafe. The baristas are super nice and very skilled, which is hard to find in a lot of the country.

I ordered a washed anaerobic Colombian from their Competition series. Sometimes these coffees can be a bit over fermented for my taste, but this was probably the best anaerobic I've ever tried. The barista brewed in an origami dripper. Such a nice acidity and balanced flavor that was sweet and fruity tasting. My wife got a hot chocolate that she very much enjoyed as well.

Coffee Project New York is hands down one of the best coffee spots I’ve ever visited in the city! The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, with the aroma of freshly brewed coffee filling the air. Their baristas are incredibly skilled and friendly — they truly care about every cup they serve. I tried their signature deconstructed latte, and it was an experience in itself — rich, smooth, and beautifully balanced. The minimalist decor and chill vibe make it perfect for relaxing or getting some work done. Definitely a must-visit for true coffee lovers in NYC!

Coffee Project in the East Village is a small, modern spot with a Euro-café vibe (bonus points for the no-laptop policy). Their medium-roast blend—Colombia meets Guatemala—hits a sweet spot: smooth milk-chocolate body with a caramel finish, zero bitterness, and plenty of flavor.

I ordered an almond croissant, and the staff offered to pop it in the oven. It came out crisp outside, soft and fragrant inside—no sogginess. They even let us enjoy a couple of pastries we’d picked up elsewhere at their outdoor table, all with genuine warmth.

Friendly people, thoughtful touches, and a medium roast I’d gladly order again. Perfect coffee-and-pastry stop in the neighborhood.

drink.name: Cardamom latte
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers specifically praise the cardamom latte for its balanced sweetness, smooth coffee, and aromatic cardamom flavor.
drink.quote: one of my absolute favorites

food.name: Pain suisse
food.reason: Praised in two reviews as flaky, chocolatey, and 'perfect'—one of the shop's most popular pastries with a 9/10 rating.
food.quote: very flakey and chocolately! Would get again

reviewSample.text:
La Cabra is absolutely worth the hype. The coffee was outstanding—smooth, rich, and perfectly balanced, the kind that reminds you why you love specialty coffee in the first place. I paired it with an almond croissant, and it was honestly so good: flaky, buttery, with just the right amount of almond filling, not too sweet and beautifully baked.
Yes, expect a line outside, especially during busy hours, but don’t let that discourage you. The wait moves fairly quickly, and once you taste the coffee and pastries, you’ll understand why people are willing to queue. It’s very much worth it.
The space itself is minimal, calm, and thoughtfully designed. There’s also a quiet resting/sitting area, which makes it a nice spot to slow down, recharge, and actually enjoy your coffee instead of rushing through it. A must-visit for coffee lovers.

This is truly one of the finest cafés in New York City. Originally from Denmark, they now have multiple locations across the city, and I’ve visited one — this one happens to be a bit more spacious. When I stopped by on a weekday afternoon, the place was buzzing with energy. There was a steady line at the counter, but the atmosphere felt cozy and lively.

I ordered my usual iced cardamom latte, which I highly recommend if you love the aroma of cardamom. The sweetness is perfectly balanced, the coffee smooth, and the milk-to-espresso ratio spot-on. That subtle hint of cardamom really elevates the flavor — it’s one of my absolute favorites.

I also picked up a ham and cheese croissant. The barista didn’t warm it up, and I didn’t ask either — but even served cold, it tasted delicious. Flaky, buttery, and satisfying enough to devour right away. I can only imagine it would be even better warm.

Overall, great coffee, good food, and an inviting atmosphere. Definitely one of my go-to café spots in NYC — I’ll absolutely be back.

They have a lot of good washed coffees. Sagastume in particular has a smooth tea like body and taste - a good everyday cup. Pricing is not terrible - $25 for 200oz but in store cups were like $12 a piece which is pretty crazy. I would come here again for tea-like coffees that are washed and tasty baked goods but I was sad there were no special or interesting fermented / funky coffees.

drink.name: Espresso
drink.reason: Specifically praised for flavor and preparation in a 5-star review; coffee program highly regarded across multiple reviews.
drink.quote: The espresso had a great flavor and it was made perfectly

food.name: Egg and cheese sandwich
food.reason: Singled out as one of the best in the city with specific praise for the chipotle mayo. Breakfast items broadly praised, but this had the strongest specific endorsement.
food.quote: one of the best egg and cheese sandwiches I've had in the city

reviewSample.text: This place. I’m obsessed!! I stopped by once for coffee and I thought I would try their egg and cheese: best decision of my life. Honestly one of the best egg and cheese sandwiches I’ve had in the city. I think the chipotle mayo has something to do with this. I assume the rest of the menu is as good but this one stuck with me. Staff is super friendly as well! And coffee’s really good too.

This place is definitely a five star specialty coffee shop with five star food. I really enjoyed the espresso. I tried one of the light roast espresso lens and also I tried the breakfast burrito. The espresso had a great flavor and it was made perfectly, and the breakfast burrito was very filling and quite delicious. I would say that the prices here are very expensiveprobably because of the location. I think the prices are worth it, but seem to be a little bit too expensive in my opinion the cost for espresso was five dollars just for a double shot of espresso.

Great selection of high end roasters. Super knowledgable and friendly staff, especially if you’re a coffee connoisseur. Shout out
Caleb for being super hospitable and nerding out with me. They brew their filter/pourovers on a Decent, and I was pleasantly surprised! Definitely will be back.

drink.name: Vanilla Latte
drink.reason: Specifically praised as 'velvety smooth' by a self-described coffee snob; flat white also named but vanilla latte received the most vivid descriptor. Matcha also highly praised by a non-coffee drinker.
drink.quote: the vanilla latte was velvety smooth

food.reason: Reviewers mention pastries and food positively but no specific food item is named in any review.

reviewSample.text: STUNNING coffee shop located on the UES. It’s spotless and has such a cozy, tranquil energy. This is the kind of coffee shop you would have a meet cute in and bump into the love of your life. The coffee and food is top notch. You can tell the owners put a lot of thought into the quality of products sold here. If you’re into flavored coffee, definitely try the syrups. They have such a wide selection and they’re small batch & organic. Service is also excellent. The guys in the front are some of the warmest people I’ve ever encountered in NYC.

The best cozy and delicious coffee shop
on the UES! I stumbled on this gem walking around the neighbourhood. The coffee is so smooth, and they were super friendly. There’s a good amount of seating indoors and outside. Fridge is stocked with goodies for kids, like good quality juices - great spot for families. Best of all, they have soy milk. It’s rare to find cafes that offer soy, and their quality made me value this place over other coffee spots in the area. Will be back and will let family and friends know to come here for their coffee!

As a major coffee snob, this place is incredible. George the owner personally greeted us and offered to help in any way, the flat white was perfect, the vanilla latte was velvety smooth, the vibe was warm & cozy, & they love dogs. Can't go wrong.

drink.name: Miruku Matcha
drink.reason: Singled out as one of the best matcha lattes by a self-proclaimed matcha snob in a 5-star review; other drink mentions are generic 'coffee.'
drink.quote: one of the best matcha lattes I've ever had

food.name: Pain au Chocolat
food.reason: Named specifically across multiple reviews with consistent praise for flaky, buttery pastry and delicious chocolate filling.
food.quote: Pain au Chocolat was crispy and the chocolate inside is delicious

reviewSample.text:
We actually stumbled upon WatchHouse 5th Ave. completely by chance — it was raining, and we were out for a walk looking for a cozy spot to dry off and warm up. What a wonderful surprise it turned out to be!

The atmosphere is modern yet calm, with beautiful design details and a warm, welcoming vibe. It feels like the kind of place you could easily spend hours in — chatting, relaxing, or simply people-watching with a cup of something amazing in hand.

The pastries looked irresistible, especially the croissants and pain au chocolat — perfectly flaky and buttery. I had the Miruku Matcha, and it was one of the best matcha lattes I’ve ever had (and I’m a self-proclaimed matcha snob!). The flavor was smooth, balanced, and clearly made with high-quality matcha. The others had coffee and chai — both equally delicious and beautifully presented.

The service was friendly and efficient, and the staff clearly took pride in what they do. Everything from the drinks to the atmosphere felt thoughtfully crafted.

Such a lovely little gem in the city — we’ll definitely be back, rain or shine. 🌧️💚☕

LOVE THE COFFEE!!!!! Perfect blend and the taste note was really standout. Pain au Chocolat was crispy and the chocolate inside is delicious. They have special blend during Christmas in the box for you to purchase. Also love the architecture and interior. The shop was really clean.

Lovely spot. Excellent coffee and pastries coupled with exemplary service. Staff had lots of smiles and were very helpful in answering questions. Plates and cups were whisked away gracefully as I was done with them. I just wish the pourovers had more affordable options. This one ran $27 and it’s not even the most expensive.

drink.name: Espresso
drink.reason: Reviewer specifically praised the espresso with rich crema and smooth, balanced finish after barista walkthrough. Other reviews generally praise the coffee as smooth and balanced but don't name specific drinks.
drink.quote: pulled beautifully with rich crema and a smooth, balanced finish

food.name: Homemade cakes and cookies
food.reason: Reviewer 4 specifically calls out homemade cakes and cookies as great dessert options. Review 1 also praises pastries as fresh and delicious, supporting the baked goods recommendation.
food.quote: Great desserts options from home made cakes and cookies

reviewSample.text:
A very cozy, welcoming spot with a genuinely homey feel. Small tables, comfortable chairs, and even a little couch that makes you want to stay longer than planned.

The coffee is done properly, smooth and well balanced, and the pastries are fresh and delicious. The service is genuinely friendly and attentive without hovering, which is rarer than it should be.

This place feels personal and relaxed, like a neighborhood café that actually cares. Easy five stars.

SOTE Coffee Roasters in NYC was an incredible experience from start to finish — the space felt intentional, calm, and genuinely crafted for people who love great coffee. The barista took time to walk me through their espresso options, and I ended up grabbing a bag of beans that pulled beautifully with rich crema and a smooth, balanced finish. If you care about quality roasting and thoughtful hospitality, SOTE absolutely delivers.

Highly recommend for coffee lovers in UWS ☕
This coffee shop has such a lovely atmosphere — it’s small, cozy, and super welcoming. You can smell the coffee from the moment you walk in. The drinks taste great, nicely bold and perfectly balanced — exactly my kind of coffee.

The owner and staff are incredibly friendly and make you feel comfortable right away. It’s a perfect spot whether you want a quick grab & go or to sit and chill with friends 🤍

Best part… it’s dog-friendly too! 🐶✨ Love this place so much.

drink.name: Lavender latte
drink.reason: Specifically named and praised as 'most delicious' in a 5-star review; iced coffee also mentioned positively but lavender latte received the most detailed praise.
drink.quote: She made me the most delicious lavender latte!

food.reason: Only one vague mention of 'the food' with no specific item named.

reviewSample.text: Stumbled upon this little coffee shop and will be a regular from now on! Abby was the sweetest and helped me decide on what to order by asking me about what I liked. She made me the most delicious lavender latte! Def come by to get your coffee fix.
Honestly the best service in the neighborhood . I work next door and I really love the food X glad my coworker put me on to it .
The people here are always so warm and friendly! Shop is super cute inside and the price is reasonable.

drink.name: Espresso
drink.reason: Multiple reviewers specifically praise the espresso and specialty coffee offerings, with one calling it 'incredible'.
drink.quote: Incredible espresso!! This place is the real deal specialty coffee shop

food.reason: Only one specific food item (black sesame pinwheel) was named, and the reviewer disliked it. Other pastries were mentioned but not tried.

reviewSample.text:
Incredible espresso!! This place is the real deal specialty coffee shop. I really enjoyed my visit and loved the space. They have very good offerings here for coffee and espresso.

They also offer an iced green tea with the espresso as a pallet cleanser in a really cool setting. You can buy a lot of varieties of freshly roasted specialty beans here as well.

What a lovely gem this coffee shop is. I go there almost every week, and every single time the coffee is consistently excellent. The staff is always friendly, and even when they are extremely busy, they manage to get the drinks out fast.

I have travelled the world extensively over 40 years. I ate at all restaurants ranging from pop & mom’s to Michelin three stars. This is my honest review of the Cafe Villager next to Brooklyn museum, NYC, USA.

A lovely coffee spot just a 4-minute walk from the Brooklyn Museum. The place has a bright, clean decor with a long bench along the wall — perfect for solo visitors or pairs. They serve excellent specialty coffee, play great music, and the restrooms are clean and well maintained. Overall, a great place to take a break while exploring this part of Brooklyn.

drink.name: Flat white
drink.reason: Only specifically named drink, described as delicious. Other reviews praise coffee generally as rich and fresh.
drink.quote: I got their flat white, it was delicious

food.name: Pastries
food.reason: Mentioned multiple times as fresh and delicious, though no specific pastry is named.
food.quote: delicious coffee and pastries

reviewSample.text:
Incredible coffee shop with delicious coffee and pastries, ample space for chatting, reading, or working, and the most pleasant staff you will find. Seriously, the people here are unbelievable kind and courteous.

Free wifi and a large communal table is perfect for solo laptop users. There’s a bathroom, too. Great place to get some work done before or after a visit to ALCOVE nearby where you can have work calls in private.

Wish I could give it 6 stars.
Great coffee and a really nice cozy spot. The coffee was rich, fresh, and perfectly made. Definitely a place I’d come back to for a good cup of coffee. ☕️

Cuppa is wonderful refuge. The cafe is clean and relaxing, offering a variety of seating whether you’re looking to relax or you’ve come to work.  WiFi is very reliable. The baristas are friendly, polite and skilled. I love the drinks and the food!  Many of the patrons are familiar because many are regulars. Definitely a testament for their feelings about the place.

drink.name: Gingerbread latte
drink.reason: Specifically praised in a 5-star review with enthusiastic detail; other lattes mentioned but this one got the most specific positive callout.
drink.quote: I thoroughly enjoyed my Gingerbread latte with 2 extra shots and cream

food.name: Egg and cheese sandwich
food.reason: Mentioned in two 5-star reviews with the highest praise, including 'best egg & cheese bagel I have had in my entire life' and detailed praise of the brioche preparation.
food.quote: the best egg & cheese bagel I have had in my entire life

reviewSample.text:
Lovely little shop putting out fantastic sandwiches. I highly recommend the egg and cheese sandwich in which they melt the cheese on the brioche so that it is easy to taste without diminishing the egg taste. The spicy turkey sandwich also has good bread that is importantly toasted to crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. The sauce again makes the sandwich far better than expected. I also really liked the yuzu cake.

This is half a block from the Plaza Hotel. If I was staying at the Plaza Hotel, I'd rather eat at Sip & Co than take lunch at the Plaza. Sorry not sorry.

I am so glad I stumbled into this NYC gem! The coffee was delicious and the atmosphere was warm and cozy. They have a little seating area out front and a window seat area with a great view! The staff was super fast and efficient, lots of options and festive decor. A must visit near the park! Great location and I thoroughly enjoyed my Gingerbread latte with 2 extra shots and cream. So YuM! So holiday!

Impeccable service, This Fine Monday Morning 3.2.26 … sweet smooth music in background , clients are here for their brew, Before work and seeking a few moments of calm enjoying a great coffee before the day starts, Suits, T-Shirts Versace and Levis all welcome it seems with this eclectic clientele it equals an Awesome vibe…and Coffee Coffee Coffee
The Montreal Tourist

My2cents

drink.name: Matcha Latte
drink.reason: Multiple 5-star reviews praise the matcha latte as strong, creamy, well-priced, and classic. One negative review exists for the strawberry variant, but the standard matcha latte is the most consistently celebrated drink.
drink.quote: Match latte was pretty a classic but creamy

food.name: Egg Tart
food.reason: The egg tart is enthusiastically praised across multiple 5-star reviews for its soft, creamy filling and flaky layers, with reviewers ordering it on repeat visits.
food.quote: Egg tart was soooo moist! Soft filling that collapsed with flaky layers

reviewSample.text: Before Coffee delivers a clean, intentional cafe experience. I had the iced matcha tea and it was refreshing, smooth, and well balanced. Service was warm and efficient, and the space felt calm and thoughtfully designed. A solid stop for quality drinks and a quick reset.

Greattttt coffee spot! Deserving of all 4.9 stars here on Google maps. The matcha was strong and great , and well priced (less than $6 matcha latte after tax).
They have some fun seasonal drinks.
Their pastries look great. Including a lot of yummy scones and an everything croissant.
Small space to sit.
Super nice staff.

This place.. Their Egg Tart……… totally a crime. Get it. Some seats for ppl to stay a little bit with great pastries and drinks. Reasonable prices which I do appreciate.

💛✨🌟Egg tart was soooo moist! Soft filling that collapsed with flaky layers outside. (If u r Taiwanese, think about a LFC egg tart but bigger and butter.)

💚Match latte was pretty a classic but creamy.

drink.name: Drip coffee
drink.reason: Coffee is praised across multiple reviews; drip coffee specifically called out as well-prepared. Pourovers available Tuesdays but less broadly reviewed.
drink.quote: The drip coffee was on point, actually being not too hot

food.name: Jambon Beurre
food.reason: Named as a top favorite in multiple 5-star reviews with specific, enthusiastic praise for the butter, mustard, and meat combination on the baguette.
food.quote: jambon beurre is the top top top recommendation

reviewSample.text:
Wonderful find in midtown. The baguettes are on point, a thin crunch on the outside with a very moist and airy medium. The jambon beurre is the top top top recommendation, with a lovely fatty butter and a tangy mustard and a thinly sliced meat that work perfectly together. The country pâte had those great moist chunks of meat and fat and flavor, soft enough for the baguette but not a paste as is often found in NYC. The drip coffee was on point, actually being not too hot with the water.

The atmosphere is great, with a front part for the quick socialization and a back part for the deeper chats. Use the front to work or to say hi quickly. If you need to give dating advice or career advice, get some coffee and sandwiches and go to the back.

The staff are excellent and ensure the area is clean, making the cafe easy to enjoy. For coffee nerds, I was told Tuesdays are when you can get the pourovers (their lineup looks legit).

Highly highly recommend, this will be a place I visit frequently as long as I work in midtown.

Pavé was such a lovely surprise in the middle of busy Midtown. It’s a small, cozy place that instantly makes you feel relaxed, a perfect escape from the usual city madness just a few steps away.

The staff is genuinely friendly and welcoming, the kind of service that makes you want to stay a bit longer. Breakfast was excellent, beautifully prepared, and the coffee was really good - exactly what you need to start a New York day properly.

We liked it so much that we actually went back a second time, which says it all. If you’re looking for a calm, charming spot with great breakfast and coffee in Midtown, Pavé is definitely worth a visit

reviewSample.text:
Hey friends! This review is about sustainability only.

I’m so grateful that you serve coffee in real, reusable mugs, upon request. But I wonder if you would make more effort to clearly ask customers if they are staying to enjoy their drink or if they are taking it to go.

By 2050, there will be more plastic in the ocean than fish. And almost 95% of the plastic that we produce and consume does not get recycled, but rather ends up in landfills, incinerators, or waterways, ultimately breaking down into microplastics that leach chemicals into our bodies.

While you have awesome reusable options, it seems that at least half of the patrons enjoying their drink to stay either were not aware of them, or don’t know to ask. Also, if I am to infer by the presence of reusable mugs and trays to collect them, I would bet you have the ability to wash water cups as well. Perhaps you could stop serving the single use plastic cups at the water station, as well? Instead, just offer real glasses or cups?

This seems like an easily solved problem. Perhaps you could communicate more clearly at the counter, or promote reusables on social. But there’s no reason to see this much single use plastic trash piling up in the receptacles, clearly from people who stayed to finish their drink.

Thank you for considering how to help your customers create less plastic, and work toward a healthier in New York City and world at large.

We were walking down Lexington Ave recently and decided to try this coffee shop.  Inside there is ample comfortable seating but many customers were using computers and not moving.  We had to wait about 15 minutes to get seating with a small shared table.
We first ordered a decaf Americano and enjoyed people watching. Then I went back and got a slice of banana bread.  They heated it up and it was delicious.
Nice vibe and unusual decoration all over with lots of plants.  We could have stayed a lot longer but had t go.  Definitely will go again.
Great area for attracting young office workers.

Quite disappointed. The reviews made the place seem great! But my cappuccino was poorly made and very bitter. I paid $9 for it—it was the “Sugar & Spice” flavor they had for the holidays. Asked for it extra hot but it was just warm, and didn’t have any flavor.

The place was also absolutely packed. I somehow managed to snag a table after waiting a few minutes.

reviewSample.text: Tiny but cozy coffee stand in Dumbo. If you go to the Brooklyn Flea on weekends, you can’t miss passing by. They have loyalty cards that you paste right there so you can mark it on your next visits. Loved the concept.

As I bit into it, I was immediately met with a strong taste of mustard, which I am allergic to. I wish the barista would have told me that it came with mustard, and I would have requested for him to take it off. To be fair, I could have asked him about the ingredients but I honestly was not expecting a “grilled cheese” to have anything other than cheese in it.

I am really sad that I wasted my money because I was unable to finish it. :(

Update: The owner offered for me to come back to try their grilled cheese without the mustard. Not only was it one of the best grilled cheese sandwiches that I’ve ever had, but the customer service was great! Along with my grilled-cheese, I was given a flat white, and it was the best damn flat white that I have ever had! Thank you Gio! Xoxo

Truly a happy accident!!! 🙏🏽❤️

Sometimes the best mornings are the ones you don’t plan. After an early subway misadventure that landed us in the Bronx instead of Battery park for coffee and sunrise… (OOPS).

My wife and I didn’t mind, We grabbed a quick breakfast sandwich, stayed positive and happy as we headed back toward Manhattan to find coffee and plan the rest of our day. As we were walking out of the subway station, we stumbled upon a small underground café that felt like pure magic.

What truly made the experience unforgettable was meeting E, the barista. He wasn’t even supposed to be working that morning, which made our encounter feel like fate. From the moment we walked in and heard him chatting with the customers we felt his energy.

We ordered and sat down. In between customers when it was quiet, we laughed, swapped stories, and totally vibed with E!!! We ended up staying nearly two hours, playing chess and having a truly fantastic experience. Exactly what we had wanted in a NYC coffee shop.

The cappuccino was absolutely phenomenal!!! And more than the delicious drinks, it was the feeling of connection and serendipity that made this café stand out.

This whole morning was a happy accident, the wrong train, the unexpected detour, and the chance meeting with E all came together to create one of the most memorable café experiences we’ve ever had.

If you ever find yourself wandering Manhattan, don’t be afraid to take a wrong turn, you might just stumble into something wonderful too.

Cheers to Happy Accidents, Fate and Love!!! ☕️

reviewSample.text: I frequent this place ALOT! I love the up-beat atmosphere/culture, and I really love how attentive and personable the baristas are. My go-to order is a Chai latte with half & half along with a Parmesan scone, and I do not have any complaints. I also own a business on Gold Street, and from one business owner to another, thank you for such good service! 💐

Stepping into this coffee shop, you're immediately greeted by a bright, inviting atmosphere that promises a delightful experience. The shelves are thoughtfully stocked with a variety of refreshing drinks, from vibrant orange juices and unique Japanese beverages to elegant bottled water, suggesting a commitment to quality and diverse offerings. Beyond the drinks, the space itself is spacious and inviting, making it perfect for settling in to work or catching up with friends.
The attention to detail extends to their branded merchandise, from stylish mugs featuring a distinctive logo to elegant Chemex coffee makers and charming tote bags, all hinting at a serious passion for coffee culture.

I’ve been working in downtown Brooklyn for over two years, and this spot has seen two coffee shops come and go—until now. This one is different!

The renovation alone makes you want to linger—it’s warm, inviting, and thoughtfully designed for both a quick coffee run or a relaxing break. The customer service? A massive upgrade. From the friendly baristas to management, everyone genuinely cares about delivering a great experience. It’s refreshing to be greeted with a smile alongside my go-to Honey Latte!

Speaking of drinks, the coffee here is top-notch. If you’re new, I can’t recommend the Honey Latte and Honey Cortado from their secret menu enough—they’re perfection.

And don’t get me started on the food! The almond croissant is heavenly, and their new toasted honey ham and cheese croissant sandwich? Pure deliciousness.

I can’t wait to see what seasonal drinks they come up with next. This coffee shop is exactly what this space—and the neighborhood—needed. Highly recommend!

reviewSample.text:
My favorite place in Midtown to hang out.

Been coming here almost every time I visit the city. It radiates a bit of that sterile corporate energy, but the covered outdoor space is really nice, and I never have to fight for a seat. It’s always spotless, and more importantly, they have a clean, accessible co-ed public restroom (which, in this neighborhood, is basically a unicorn).

Drink-wise, they specialize in flavored lattes like Vanilla Date and Butterscotch. The latter is my go-to, and while it is aggressively sweet, it oddly reminds me of Butterbeer from the Harry Potter theme parks, which I used to love.

Probably not the best spot this time of year, but when the weather’s nice, it’s definitely one of the best outdoor space to chill in the area.

Yes, cappuccino was great. Yes a tad too expensive for NYC standards but you know… it’s a nice area.
The place is an open atrium that is classy and gives good vibes.
Obviously it is a privately owned public space .
The restroom next to the counter (last pic, glass door) might be the most visually appealing I have ever seen in NYC

Black Fox Coffee, NYC — it’s a mood, but not a sip

Walked into Black Fox with curiosity — I’d seen it on a few “best NYC cafés” lists and finally decided to check it out.
The interior? Absolutely gorgeous. Minimalist, modern, full of light — the kind of place where you want to slow down, open a book, or just watch the world pass by.

So I ordered a vanilla latte.

And here’s the thing: I wanted to love it.
But the coffee itself just didn’t land for me. It felt overly sweet, muted, and lacked that deep, warm comfort I look for in a great cup.
Kind of like when you meet someone beautiful but the conversation doesn’t click. Still polite, still pleasant — just not the connection you expected.

The pastry I tried was fine, but small and not memorable. I left a little underwhelmed — and wishing the flavor matched the aesthetics.

Why people love this place: the vibe. It’s serene, it’s well-designed, and yes, it looks great on camera.
If someone paired this space with a roastery that really knew how to hit the palate? Honestly, it would be iconic.

Would I come back? Maybe — to meet someone, to sit and journal, to enjoy the space. But not for the coffee.
That said, I respect the effort. I respect the space. And I hope they keep evolving.

— Ilya
#RecommendsTheAtmosphere #NotTheLatte
#CyborgTastesNYC #CoffeeJournal #BlackFoxReview

reviewSample.text: My wife and I visited NYC during a blizzard and stopped to warm up with coffee and an almond milk matcha. They are top atmosphere is fun, coffee tasty and high quality, and the matcha was very healthy and could use some added sugar to sweeten it up. The baristas were awesome and provided fun and great service. We really enjoyed our first time to Gumptown.

I was looking for a local coffee spot on my way to my collaborative book release in times square and passed Gumption Coffee. I ordered and they gave my regular black drip back pretty quickly. It tasted full and slightly bitter and not burnt and acidic like that nasty chain, which I appreciate. Viva los local coffee shops!

Gumption clearly prides itself on deep coffee knowledge and serious experience — and they make sure you know it. Unfortunately, the focus seems to lean more toward coffee than toward people.

I had a cortado, which is usually my benchmark for judging bean quality, extraction, and latte art. Sadly, none of the three really stood out. Perfectly drinkable, but far from memorable — especially considering the reputation.

But here’s the bigger issue: coffee simply never tastes as good when the people serving it make you feel like you’re not particularly welcome in their shop. Expertise is great; hospitality is better.

Too bad, because the potential is clearly there.

La Belle Tesse Coffee Shop is an absolute delight and a wonderful new addition to the neighborhood. From the moment you approach the entrance, the flowers at the entry set the tone — welcoming, artistic, and instantly charming. The thought put into the design is clear throughout. Inside, the shaded colors on the walls pair beautifully with the décor, and the furniture matches the floral theme, creating a space that feels carefully curated yet warm and inviting. The whole atmosphere almost transports you to an era bygone, where sitting down for coffee or tea feels like an experience, not just a quick stop.

The drinks were excellent. I tried both the tea and coffee, and each was rich, flavorful, and clearly made with care. What really caught my eye was the cup and plate showcase — such a unique and creative touch that added to the character of the café. Details like this show how much effort has gone into making the space not just a coffee shop but a spot where design and hospitality come together.

Service was friendly and welcoming, adding to the overall relaxed vibe.

Overall, La Belle Tesse is more than just a coffee shop — it’s a beautifully designed, cozy space with excellent drinks and a unique charm. A great new spot in the neighborhood that I’ll definitely return to.

reviewSample.text:
We stopped into Drip on Thanksgiving morning and it felt like such a gift. It is a beautiful, intentional coffee shop tucked inside a gorgeous building, with plenty of comfortable lobby seating where you can actually sit, exhale, and enjoy your cup instead of rushing out the door. Everything feels thoughtfully curated, from the layout to the clean, minimalist bar, to the option to enjoy your drink for here or take it to go as you wander SoHo.

We had the pleasure of meeting the owners, a couple who left the corporate world to build something with real meaning. You can feel that this place is not driven by money, it is driven by purpose, craft, and care. They walked us through the different beans, from lighter, brighter roasts all the way to deeper, darker options, and helped us pick out a bag of Black & White beans to take home. Our coffees were pulled on a beautiful Strada, perfectly dialed in, with silky, glossy milk and latte art that was so precise it lined up with the handle, with perfect contrast in the cup.

What stayed with me most was hearing their story and seeing how deeply they dive into everything they take on. Their knowledge of coffee is encyclopedic, yet their vibe is grounded, humble, and warm. It was genuinely humbling to hear about their journey from corporate to coffee and to experience how that shows up in every detail of the shop. I cannot wait to cross paths with them again at Specialty Coffee Association or Coffee Fest one day, and we will definitely be back the next time we are in New York.

Incredible pour overs and espresso! I travel all over and try and find shops that can do these things well. This is hands down the best I've had.  The fruit snacks blend from Black and White was unique and delicious. I took some of the tips I got from the staff here with a couple of bags of coffee and V60 papers home, and I have absolutely improved my home pour overs.  I can't wait to come back and see what they have to offer next time I'm in New York.

This place is exceptional on every level. I ordered a pour over and a flat white—both were perfect. The flavor, texture, and balance were spot on. You can tell they take coffee seriously here.

What truly sets this spot apart, though, are the people. Gio and Chris are incredibly knowledgeable, generous with their time, and genuinely kind. They offered great recommendations and answered all my questions without hesitation. Their passion for coffee and hospitality really shines through.

Will definitely make it a point to visit here anytime I’m in NY!
